LAW AND MOTION 301, A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF MORGAN, LEWIS & BOCKIUS, LLP MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT WHETHER STRANGER AGREED TO BE BOUND BY THE CONTRACT INDIVIDUALLY IN LIGHT OF AMBIGUITY IN THE CONTRACT AND CONFLICTING EXTRINSIC EVIDENCE. THERE IS ALSO A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT WHETHER THE REQUEST TO REPRESENT STRANGER INDIVIDUALLY WAS MADE ONLY ON BEHALF OF THE BANKRUPT ENTITY. EVEN ASSUMING PLAINTIFF HAS SHOWN WORK BENEFITING DEFENDANT AT HIS REQUEST, HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN A PORTION OF ITS WORK BENEFITING STRANGER AS OPPOSED TO THE BANKRUPT ENTITY, AND IN ANY EVENT A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO THE REASONABLE AMOUNT OF FEES. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT THE DEFENDANT HAS A DUTY TO DISCLOSE OR THE MATERIALITY OF THE NON-DISCLOSURE WHICH RESTS ENTIRELY ON CHIPPEY'S DECLARATION WHICH THE COURT DISREGARDS AS TO THIS POINT UNDER C C P SECTION 437 C (E). OBJECTIONS WERE RULED ON THE RECORD. MR. STRANGER TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G). JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189 301/PJB

MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RYAN L. SCHER & ATTY. BRETT M. SCHUMAN OF THE MORGAN & LEWIS LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 442-1222 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
GREG STRANGER, TEL. NO. (239) 537-3377, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF MORGAN, LEWIS & BOCKIUS, LLP MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THERE IS A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT WHETHER STRANGER AGREED TO BE BOUND BY THE CONTRACT INDIVIDUALLY IN LIGHT OF AMBIGUITY IN THE CONTRACT AND CONFLICTING EXTRINSIC EVIDENCE. THERE IS ALSO A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT WHETHER THE REQUEST TO REPRESENT STRANGER INDIVIDUALLY WAS MADE ONLY ON BEHALF OF THE BANKRUPT ENTITY. EVEN ASSUMING PLAINTIFF HAS SHOWN WORK BENEFITING DEFENDANT AT HIS REQUEST, HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN A PORTION OF ITS WORK BENEFITING STRANGER AS OPPOSED TO THE BANKRUPT ENTITY, AND IN ANY EVENT A TRIABLE ISSUE OF MATERIAL FACT AS TO THE REASONABLE AMOUNT OF FEES. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT THE DEFENDANT HAS A DUTY TO DISCLOSE OR THE MATERIALITY OF THE NON-DISCLOSURE WHICH RESTS ENTIRELY ON CHIPPEY'S DECLARATION WHICH THE COURT DISREGARDS AS TO THIS POINT UNDER C C P SECTION 437 C (E). OBJECTIONS WERE RULED ON THE RECORD. MR. STRANGER TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G). Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB
